listOfQuestions.question: In Genetics and Heredity, what does "DNA" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A molecule that stores hereditary instructions
wrongAnswers: A long DNA structure containing many genes
An allele expressed when two copies are present
Copying DNA information into RNA

listOfQuestions.question: In Genetics and Heredity, what does "Gene" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A DNA segment that influences a trait or product
wrongAnswers: A version of a gene
Having two matching alleles for a gene
Building a protein using RNA instructions

listOfQuestions.question: In Genetics and Heredity, what does "Chromosome" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A long DNA structure containing many genes
wrongAnswers: The genetic makeup for a trait
Having two different alleles for a gene
RNA that carries genetic instructions from DNA to ribosomes

listOfQuestions.question: In Genetics and Heredity, what does "Allele" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A version of a gene
wrongAnswers: The observable expression of a trait
A change in DNA sequence
A diagram used to predict possible inherited traits

listOfQuestions.question: In Genetics and Heredity, what does "Genotype" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: The genetic makeup for a trait
wrongAnswers: An allele expressed when one copy is present
The process of making proteins from genetic instructions
An individual who has one recessive disease allele without showing the trait

listOfQuestions.question: In Genetics and Heredity, what does "Phenotype" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: The observable expression of a trait
wrongAnswers: An allele expressed when two copies are present
Copying DNA information into RNA
A three-base RNA sequence that specifies an amino acid or stop signal

listOfQuestions.question: In Genetics and Heredity, what does "Dominant allele" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: An allele expressed when one copy is present
wrongAnswers: Having two matching alleles for a gene
Building a protein using RNA instructions
All genetic material in an organism

listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: An allele expressed when two copies are present
wrongAnswers: Having two different alleles for a gene
RNA that carries genetic instructions from DNA to ribosomes
Differences in DNA among individuals

wrongAnswers: A change in DNA sequence
A diagram used to predict possible inherited traits
A molecule that stores hereditary instructions

wrongAnswers: The process of making proteins from genetic instructions
An individual who has one recessive disease allele without showing the trait
A DNA segment that influences a trait or product

wrongAnswers: Copying DNA information into RNA
A three-base RNA sequence that specifies an amino acid or stop signal
A long DNA structure containing many genes

All genetic material in an organism

wrongAnswers: RNA that carries genetic instructions from DNA to ribosomes
